verdict_reasoning: LYN is a Src-family non-receptor tyrosine kinase anchored exclusively to the cytoplasmic (inner) leaflet of the plasma membrane via dual N-terminal lipid modifications: myristoylation at Gly-2 and palmitoylation at Cys-3. It has no transmembrane domain, no GPI anchor, and no extracellular domain. All signaling activity occurs on the cytosolic face. Mutations in both acylation sites delocalize it to cytosol/nucleus. No documented outer-leaflet, ecto-surface, or cell-death-induced outer-leaflet pool is reported in the literature. No surface-directed therapeutic (antibody/ADC/CAR-T) targets the LYN protein body from outside the cell.
